summary: coral-wraith postinstall exfiltrates recon and SSH key results to hardcoded remote IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes a shell pipeline that port-scans the internal gateway (172.16.16.0/24), curls internal services, reads /root/.ssh/id_rsa and uses it to attempt SSH login as root and git across ports 22/2222/8022/443/32271, greps the filesystem for HTB{...} strings, then base64-encodes the collected output and HTTP PUTs it to a hardcoded bare-IP endpoint at http://154.57.164.64:32271 (with a 127.0.0.1 fallback). Execution is gated by an evasion check (postinstall.js:21-29) that suppresses on CI/GitHub Actions/mirror registries and only fires when the hostname is a 12-hex-char container ID and the cwd contains /app — a CTF/lab targeting shape designed to hide from scanners. The combination of installer-secret read (/root/.ssh/id_rsa), lateral SSH attempts, internal network recon, hardcoded bare-IP C2, and scanner-evasion gating is unambiguous supply-chain attack behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
